name: Rodney Dangerfield
abstract: Jack Roy (born Jacob Cohen; November 22, 1921 – October 5, 2004), better known by the stage name Rodney Dangerfield, was an American stand-up comedian, actor, screenwriter, and producer. He was known for his self-deprecating one-liner humor, his catchphrase "I don't get no respect!", and his monologues on that theme.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Jazz Stars at Midnight
Rodney Dangerfield, Shlomo Carlebach, Ron Eliran, and The Ruach Revival Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Ben Sidran
about_quotes.evidence: The worst gig I ever played was as the opening act for Rodney Dangerfield... I’m going to go play my opening set as Ben Sidran but then I’m going to come back out on stage as the piano player “Styles Bitchley”... and play behind him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ben Sidran interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 15, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: I had the nerve to go up and I said, “Rodney, what are you doing?”
